title: Live Aluminum Prices Today - CarbonCredits.com
summary: Global aluminum prices rallied 1.49% to $3,604.1/ton, driven by a 'black swan' supply shock in the Middle East. A maritime blockade in the Strait of Hormuz and targeted strikes on UAE and Bahraini smelters have severely disrupted regional output, with the region representing 9% of global capacity. Strict Chinese production caps and critically low Western inventories are further amplifying the scarcity premium.

title: Aluminum - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News - Trading Economics
summary: Aluminum rose to 3,629 USD/T on April 22, 2026, up 2.73% from the previous day, with futures in the UK moving past $3,600 per tonne near a four-year high. The continued blockage of the Strait of Hormuz threatens prolonged disruption to Middle Eastern supply. Recent signals of restored vessel flows through the strait have provided some relief, with prices dipping from the three-year high of $3,670 recorded on April 16th.

title: Steel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: Steel prices fell to 3,129 CNY/T on April 21, 2026, down 0.29% from the previous day, though up 3.71% year-over-year. Steel rebar futures traded near a three-week high at around CNY 3,130 per ton due to firm seasonal demand and restocking. China Steel Corp. raised domestic prices for next month by NT$1,000 to NT$1,200 per ton, its fifth consecutive increase.

title: HRC Steel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: HRC Steel rose to 1,107.10 USD/T on April 21, 2026, up 0.46% from the previous day and the highest since January 2024. Over the past 4 weeks, prices gained 4.99%. This reflects positive momentum in hot-rolled coil steel markets.

title: Chilean miner Codelco’s El Teniente smelter to restart after collapse
summary: Chilean miner Codelco’s smelter at its El Teniente mine is set to restart on Thursday after a collapse. This development could impact copper supply from one of the world's largest producers. The restart follows recent operational disruptions in the region.

title: Copper price: Goldman maintains year-end forecast, Traxys sees $15,000
summary: Goldman Sachs maintains its year-end copper price forecast amid ongoing market volatility. Traxys predicts prices could reach $15,000 per ton. The article discusses divergent analyst views on copper market trends.

title: Nickel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: Nickel prices rose to 18,475 USD/T on April 22, 2026, up 1.79% from the previous day, reaching the highest since March 2026 amid supply constraints and cost pressures. Indonesia revised its nickel ore benchmark pricing formula, incorporating additional metals and raising the corrective factor, tightening market conditions. Prices have stabilized in the $17,000–$17,400 range due to elevated inventories and projected 2026 surplus, though Western Australia offers loans to boost production.

title: Live Nickel Prices Today
summary: Global nickel prices edged down 0.60% to $18,135.31 per ton, while advancing 0.64% to $18,360.64 per ton in recent updates, driven by supply tightening in Indonesia. Revisions to Indonesia's nickel ore pricing formula have raised upstream costs, with surging sulphur prices trimming HPAL output. Macro support from Chinese bonds and a weaker US dollar offsets elevated inventories.

title: Green nickel for sustainable electrification: Carbon-free process extracts nickel for batteries and steel
summary: Researchers at the Max Planck Institute for Sustainable Materials have developed a carbon-free, energy-saving method for nickel extraction using hydrogen plasma. This single-step process enables the use of low-grade nickel ores, reducing emissions, energy consumption, and costs while broadening usable ore sources. The results are published in Nature, with plans to scale up for industrial applications.

title: Innovations in Sustainable Nickel Production: The Path to Low-Cost Solutions
summary: The global nickel market is experiencing rapid changes with innovations in mining and refining. These advancements aim to reduce production costs and enhance environmental sustainability. The article discusses the path to low-cost, sustainable nickel solutions.

title: Prysmian Eyes New Texas Copper Mill Amid Data Center Boom
summary: Prysmian SpA, the world's largest cable maker, is considering building a new mill in Texas while pursuing U.S. acquisitions. The expansion is driven by surging demand tied to the data center boom.

title: Prysmian creates the world's first negative-carbon-footprint cable
summary: Prysmian announced the creation of the world's first negative-carbon-footprint cable, representing a significant advancement in sustainable cable manufacturing.
